title: Trading with logic vs. emotion: How I used AI to re-evaluate a "broken" structure on Gold ($XAUUSD)
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
We’ve all been there: you have a high-conviction setup, you get stopped out, and your rules tell you to walk away. But sometimes, the market is just clearing liquidity before the real move.

I posted a few hours ago about a trade I was watching on Gold. I used my AI tool (**Hercul Market**) to filter the noise.

* **Image 1 (The Setup):** The IA identified a specific demand zone after a liquidity sweep. Even though the price action looked messy, the structural logic remained bullish.
* **Image 2 (The Result):** Fast forward a few hours, and the price respected the zone perfectly, hitting the first target.

**The lesson?** Following rules is good, but following **logic** is better.

I built this AI to help me stay objective when my emotions are telling me to revenge trade or to be too scared to re-enter. It’s not about "predicting the future," it’s about confirming if the institutional order flow is still on your side.

If you're tired of second-guessing your entries, you can check out the tool here:[hercul-market-web.vercel.app](https://hercul-market-web.vercel.app/)

I’m looking for feedback from other SMC/Price Action traders. Does this automated zone look accurate to your manual analysis?
